close_reason: Duplicate of crate-secretsx-6xym.9 (same bug: k8s 403 mapped to Unavailable)
description: In secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs line 82, map_kube_error maps HTTP 403 Forbidden to SecretError::Unavailable. Unavailable implies the error is transient and retrying may help. But 403 is a permanent RBAC/permission error — the cluster has denied access and no amount of retrying will succeed until an administrator changes the RBAC policy. Callers with retry-on-Unavailable logic will spin indefinitely on a misconfigured cluster. The correct mapping is SecretError::Backend (permanent, non-retriable). By contrast, the 429 and 5xx mappings on lines 87-90 are correct (those are genuinely transient).
title: k8s: 403 Forbidden mapped to Unavailable
